# Launch Comms — ready to send

Copy for launch day. Fill the `{{placeholders}}`, keep the trademark line, send.
Voice: a practicing PE who built the tool they wanted — specific, no hype.

---

## 1. Beta / waitlist launch email

**Subject:** HydroComplete for Civil 3D is live — your beta access inside

**Preview:** Stormwater analysis, straight from the drawing. Here's your Pro token.

Hi {{first_name}},

You joined the HydroComplete waitlist — it's live on the Autodesk App Store today.

HydroComplete runs stormwater hydrology and hydraulics **straight from your Civil
3D drawing**: reads your pipe networks and catchments, computes capacity, HGL,
rational and SCS hydrology, detention, water quality, and BMP sizing — 52 commands
— and shows every formula, line by line. No re-typing geometry into a separate
model, no black boxes.

**Get started (2 minutes):**
1. Install from the Autodesk App Store: {{store_link}}
2. Launch Civil 3D 2024, 2025, or 2026 — HydroComplete auto-loads.
3. Type `HC_ABOUT` to see the commands, or open the **HydroComplete › Analysis**
   ribbon tab.

**Your Pro access:** as a beta member, here's a Pro token for sealable PDF reports:

> **Token:** `{{hc_live_token}}`
> Activate in Civil 3D: run `HC_ACTIVATE`, enter your email, paste the token.

Everything else is free forever — capacity checks, HGL, rational Q, live NOAA
Atlas 14 IDF, and formula-transparent HTML reports.

I built this for the routine sizing-and-reporting work that eats our days. If it
saves you an afternoon, tell me — and if something's off, reply straight to this
email; it reaches me.

{{your_name}}, PE
hydrocomplete.com/civil3d

*Civil 3D is a trademark of Autodesk, Inc. HydroComplete is an independent product,
not affiliated with or endorsed by Autodesk.*

---

## 2. LinkedIn post

Stormwater analysis still leaves the Civil 3D model. You export to a separate
tool, re-key the geometry, run the numbers, and hand-reconcile results back into
the drawing — every time the network changes.

I got tired of that, so I built **HydroComplete for Civil 3D**. It reads your pipe
networks and catchments straight from the open drawing and computes in place:
Manning capacity, normal-depth HGL with HEC-22 losses, Rational and SCS hydrology,
detention, water quality, BMP sizing — 52 commands — and it shows the equation
behind every number, so the report is something you can hand a plan reviewer.

Public-domain methods (Manning, Rational, NOAA Atlas 14, SCS/NRCS, HEC-22, FHWA
HDS-5). Free tier + Pro. Live on the Autodesk App Store for Civil 3D 2024/2025/2026.

If you do storm-sewer sizing or drainage reports in Civil 3D, I'd love your take:
{{store_link}}

#CivilEngineering #Stormwater #Civil3D #LandDevelopment #StormwaterManagement

---

## 3. Autodesk Civil 3D community forum post

**Title:** New App Store add-in: HydroComplete — stormwater analysis from the drawing

Hi all — I've published **HydroComplete for Civil 3D** on the Autodesk App Store
and wanted to share it here for feedback from people who live in pipe networks.

It reads pipe networks and catchments directly from the drawing and runs Manning
capacity, normal-depth HGL (with optional HEC-22 junction/exit losses and a profile
polyline), Rational + SCS hydrology, detention, and water-quality/BMP sizing — then
writes results back as labels and formula-transparent HTML reports. The design goal
was reviewability: every result carries the equation and the numbers behind it.

It supports Civil 3D 2024/2025/2026, auto-loads (no NETLOAD for end users), and has
a genuinely useful free tier. Methods are all public-domain and cited.

Honest scope: it's not trying to replace a full hydraulic-modeling suite — it
targets the everyday sizing, HGL checks, and defensible reporting where the
friction is transcription and review. Would love feedback, especially edge cases in
your real drawings.

Link: {{store_link}} · More: hydrocomplete.com/civil3d

*(Independent product; not affiliated with or endorsed by Autodesk.)*

## 4. Autodesk partnership / ADN outreach (optional warm note)

Use if you want to open a partnership conversation alongside the self-serve
listing. Send to an Autodesk Platform Services / App Store / Civil 3D product
contact — ideally via a warm intro.

**Subject:** HydroComplete — Civil 3D stormwater add-in, now on the App Store

Hi {{contact_name}},

I'm the developer of **HydroComplete for Civil 3D**, just published on the Autodesk
App Store. It brings stormwater hydrology/hydraulics into the Civil 3D drawing —
reading pipe networks and catchments directly and producing formula-transparent,
reviewable reports on public-domain methods. It supports 2024/2025/2026 as a native
auto-load add-in.

It's built to *complement* Civil 3D — making the drawing the source of truth for
drainage analysis rather than pulling work into a separate app — and it fills a
transparency gap for the everyday sizing-and-reporting tasks the drainage user base
does constantly.

I'd welcome a short conversation about deeper integration and reaching Civil 3D's
drainage users. Happy to give a 15-minute demo. Overview attached
(`docs/ONE-PAGER.md`).

Thanks for the time,
{{your_name}}, PE · hydrocomplete.com/civil3d

### Send checklist

- [ ] Replace every `{{placeholder}}` (store link, tokens, names).
- [ ] Generate a **unique** `hc_live_*` token per waitlist recipient (don't reuse one).
- [ ] Confirm `{{store_link}}` is the live App Store URL (only after approval).
- [ ] Keep the trademark/independence line on public posts.
- [ ] Stagger the waitlist email (warm up sending reputation); post LinkedIn +
      forum the same day as the store goes live.
